impact_on_program.A_SP_path: Pay |A_SP|≤17*P_multi or ≤N_ord; need P_multi or N_ord bound
impact_on_program.mark_path: No tested E2/E5 mark works on toys; need ledger pivot geometry
impact_on_program.next: Bound P_multi(z) or design injective R_sing mark from rank/RIM

OPEN.falsifier: A fiber with |A_SP| larger than the claimed U_A_SP; or two R_sing supports with the same claimed mark.
OPEN.name: OPEN_A_SP_COST_AND_R_SING_MARK
OPEN.statement:
(1) Bound max P_multi or max |A_SP| by a printed constant (ideally ≤ t·p), using SP/BC geometry — establishes A_SP as a paid cell.
(2) Prove an injective mark on R_sing into size ≤ t·p or n·p (ledger pivot / RIM / selector), not a failed local (min,·) form.

P_multi_criterion.name: P_multi_bound_gives_A_SP_cost
P_multi_criterion.open: Bound max_z P_multi(z) (number of multi-member top-seam pencils).
P_multi_criterion.statement: If max_z P_multi(z) ≤ floor(B / 17), then max |A_SP(z)| ≤ B by cost identity (i). In particular B = t·p gives the criterion P_multi ≤ floor(t·p / 17) = 8456648496904.

R_sing_mark.name: R_sing_mark_equals_core_injection
proof: Singleton pencil ⇒ unique support per core in the fiber (v15/v16).
R_sing_mark.statement: On R_sing(z), C_can: R_sing → C_res is bijective. Therefore an injective mark on residual supports exists with codomain L iff residual can-cores inject into L. E2/E5 marks may be defined on supports or cores interchangeably for R_sing.

cost_identities.name: A_SP_printed_cost_identities
proof: (i) Each multi-member pencil has size k ≤ pack_ceil by core-pencil packing (v2). Sum of k over P_multi pencils ≤ pack_ceil · P_multi.
(ii) k≤k(k-1) for k≥2: for k=2 equality; for k≥3, k-1≥2 so k(k-1)≥2k>k.
Summing over multi-member pencils yields |A_SP| ≤ N_ord.
cost_identities.statement:
Let pencils of sizes k_1,...,k_r include multi-member ones with sizes k≥2. Write
  |A_SP(z)| = sum_{k≥2} k
  P_multi(z) = |{pencils : k≥2}|
  N_ord(z) = sum_{k≥2} k(k-1)   (ordered top-seam pairs in G_z).
Then:
  (i)  |A_SP(z)| ≤ pack_ceil · P_multi(z)
       (deployed pack_ceil=17, since k ≤ floor((n-|C|)/e) ≤ 17);
  (ii) |A_SP(z)| ≤ N_ord(z)
       because for every integer k≥2 one has k ≤ k(k-1).

first_match_split.name: first_match_A_SP_cell_then_residual_sing
first_match_split.note: This packages A_SP as a payable first-match cell with an explicit support set. The missing inputs are (a) U_A_SP ≤ printed budget and (b) injective mark / mass bound on R_sing.
proof: First-match upper ledger (grande_finale): sum of cell bounds upper-bounds union. Partition Fib = A_SP ⊔ R_sing.
v14: t·p and n·p fit under TARGET.
first_match_split.statement:
In a first-match ledger, pay cell C_A_SP with E ⊇ A_SP(z) for every z and bound |E|≤U_A_SP. The residual family after this cell may be taken as R ⊆ R_sing (supports not in A_SP). Then H_seam holds on R, |R|=N_can_prim(R), and
  max |Fib residual| ≤ U_A_SP + max |R_sing|.
If U_A_SP ≤ t·p and max |R_sing| ≤ t·p (or n·p), residual-scale mass fits E2/E5 budgets (whether or not the full Q residual equals R_sing).

mark_bank.name: no_tested_E2_E5_mark_injective
mark_bank.statement: On A_SP residual R_sing (singleton top-seam pencils) for small dyadic rows, none of the following n·p-scale marks is injective on all tested rows: (min S, a_{w+1}), (min U, a_{w+1}), (min C, b1/c0/sum/prod), (sum S, a_{w+1}), (prod U, a_{w+1}), (min S, sum S). The n·p² mark (min S, a_{w+1}, a_{w+2}) is injective on some but not all toys, and n·p² is not an E2/E5 budget. Conclusion: plain local marks are unlikely to prove E2/E5; need ledger-specific pivots or a different label geometry.

partition.name: fiber_partition_A_SP_and_singleton
proof: Every support has a unique lex can-core and lies in U(C,z) for that core.
Pencils partition the fiber. Multi vs singleton is a partition of pencils.
Singleton pencils have no internal top-seam edge.
partition.statement:
For each z, partition Fib_w(z) by top-seam core pencils U(C,z):
  A_SP(z)  = union of multi-member pencils (|U(C,z)|≥2)
  R_sing(z)= union of singleton pencils (|U(C,z)|=1)
These are disjoint and cover Fib_w(z). R_sing is matching-free (H_seam) by construction. A_SP is exactly the support set assigned by rule A_SP (v16).

title: A_SP printed cost algebra; R_sing mark search bank
